chemicals.description: Plant pitcher structure may cause mild mechanical irritation if ingested.
chemicals.name: Mechanical Irritant

description: Cobra Lily (Darlingtonia californica) is a carnivorous pitcher plant native to boggy areas of Northern California and Oregon. Named for its hooded, cobra-like appearance, it traps insects in fluid-filled pitchers. Cobra Lily is not significantly toxic to cats — the digestive enzymes inside the pitchers are not harmful if a cat touches or licks the plant. The main concern is physical: the pitcher liquid is stagnant and bacteria-laden, and cats curious enough to drink from the pitchers may experience mild stomach upset.
emergencyNote: Confirmation of Species (Rule Out True Lily)
name: Cobra Lily
safetyNotes: Risk level: Low. Not documented as causing significant toxicity in cats.
Physical concern: The trapped insect liquid inside pitchers may cause mild GI upset if ingested; the pointed hood tip can cause minor scratches.
Action: Monitor for vomiting or diarrhea if the cat has interacted closely with the plant. Veterinary attention is rarely needed.

treatments.description: The veterinarian or pet owner must positively identify the plant as Darlingtonia californica. This distinguishes it from True Lilies (like Tiger, Easter, or Daylilies), which cause fatal acute kidney failure.
treatments.name: Confirmation of Species (Rule Out True Lily)
treatments.notes: This is the most critical step. If there is any doubt about the plant's identity, or if it could be a True Lily, the cat must be treated immediately for kidney failure (aggressive fluid therapy and decontamination). However, confirmed Cobra Lilies do not pose this risk.Clinical Monitoring (Home Obse

treatments.description: Monitor the cat at home for minor signs of stomach upset, such as temporary vomiting or diarrhea, which can occur after eating any plant material due to indigestible fiber.
treatments.name: Observation for Mild Gastrointestinal Upset
treatments.notes: The Cobra Lily (also known as the California Pitcher Plant) appears on lists of plants non-toxic to cats.
